name: Horseradish Butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Blend together the prepared horseradish and butter until well combined.
2. Use the mixture as a spread for canapes.
3. Garnish as desired.
Note: The International Horseradish Festival is held in Collinsville, IL every May. Sixty percent of the world's horseradish is grown there. It is relatively easy to grow. Why is it hot? Highly volatile oils are released by enzyme activity when the root cells are crushed. To grind: Wash and peel dice into small cubes. Process in a blender with enough cold water to just cover. Add 2-3 tablespoons of white vinegar and 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Process no more than half of the blender jar at a time. The amount of vinegar added is critical as it determines how hot the mixture will be. If you like it hot, wait up to 3 minutes before adding the vinegar, which stops the enzymatic action.
